titlestmt.titleproper: Cornell University. Fine Arts Library records15-3-2284
titlestmt.author: Compiled by Julia Parker

publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-05-31 18:03:56 UTC.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.unittitle: Cornell University. Fine Arts Library records
did.abstract: Records of the Fine Arts Library, 1962-1969; correspondence, 1945-1980; volumes with list of subject headings, catalogue by authors (2 v.), "Gift Book," "Classification System," storage record book, "Catalogue de la Bibliotheque d'Architecture," "References to Illustrations in the Architecture Library," and "Senior Seminary in Architectural Bibliography, 1915-1916." Also, photographs of the interior of the Fine Arts Library, book charge card, and invitation to a reception for the Cornell Journal of Architecture, n.d. Also, 1 drawing each of proposed renovations, 1974, 1977.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

arrangement.head: SERIES LIST
arrangement.p: Series VIII. New York Urban Planning Box 10
Series IX. Architecture and Design Box 10

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Cornell University. Fine Arts Library records, #15-3-2284. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: Records of the Fine Arts Library, 1962-1969; correspondence, 1945-1995; annual reports, 1967-1989; volumes with list of subject headings, catalogue by authors (2 v.), "Gift Book," "Classification System," storage record book, "Catalogue de la Bibliotheque d'Architecture," "References to Illustrations in the Architecture Library," and "Senior Seminary in Architectural Bibliography, 1915-1916." Also, photographs of the interior of the Fine Arts Library, book charge card, and invitation to a reception for the Cornell Journal of Architecture, n.d. Also, 1 drawing each of proposed renovations, 1974, 1977.

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: "List of Tompkins County Atlases and Maps in the Cornell University Library which Show Buildings," February 1964, by Gary L. Menges.

controlaccess.subject: Art libraries -- New York (State) -- Ithaca.
controlaccess.corpname: Cornell University. College of Architecture, Art, and Planning

did.unittitle: College of Architecture librarians note book classification system
References to Illustrations in the Architecture Library of the Cornell University
FAL description in Handbook of Libraries
College of Architecture Library: List of subject headings as of September 1, 1941
Original Grant Proposal/Council on Library Resources (19th Century American Architectural Periodicals Research Project)
Serials Currently Received in the Planning Collection
Newspapers Clippings pertaining to the Plaster Casts

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists mainly of museum exhibition fliers and pamphlets, but also contains catalogues, magazines, newspaper clippings, budget reports, blank postcards, resumes, a book, and biographical information pertaining to the artists and architects on file. Some personal notes and autographs from the artists are included. The labeled contents range in date from 1929 to 2007, although many items are undated.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains a signature and personal note from artist Judith Bernstein.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains a signature with a personal note from artist Jack Bosson.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains only the first issue of The Cornell Review from Spring 1977 and a newspaper clipping about Blum.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains biographical information about Alfred D. Crimi.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains biographical information about steel artist Aristides Demetrios.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains several articles about Margery Edwards.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains biographical information about artist Josep Grau-Garriga.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains a cover letter and portfolio from Fred Hurt Winston.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains only newspaper columns by and about Ada Louise Huxtable.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains biographical information and photos of completed projects.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Barnett Newman- Thomas B. Hess -1969

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains an autographed, limited edition of We The People by Joyce Cutler Shaw.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains a folder of 1960-1970 Taramind Fellowship artists whose surnames do not necessarily begin with "T."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains an exhibition pamphlet signed by artist Abraham Walkowitz.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains gallery exhibition pamphlets, museum visitor guides, annual gallery magazines, newspaper clippings, prints, memoranda, catalogues, fact sheets about exhibits, and related but miscellaneous items. In many cases, the exhibit pamphlets are undated.

did.unittitle: Albany Institute of History and Art
All India Fine Arts and Crafts

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains one pamphlet with the 1977 Distinguished Building Awards.

did.unittitle: Andrew Dickson White Museum of Art

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains an overview of the Andrew Dickson White Museum from 1963-1964.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Magazine of Columbia University

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Describes exhibitions in Memorial Room of Willard Straight Hall, Morse Hall, White Hall, and various other Cornell buildings.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains an exhibit (pencils, etchings, oil paintings) of Cornell University graduate Gillian Pederson-Krag in the Franklin Hall Gallery.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains Martha van Rensselaer Gallery exhibition calendars for the years 1952-1954 and 1965-1967, along with specific exhibition pamphlets.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains catalogues of an exhibition for water colors, an exhibition of student work, a local artists show, contemporary American drawings, war posters from 1914-1920, lithographs, wood sculptures, British watercolors, and a Cornell University faculty show in 1946.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains one exhibition booklet from the Watson Art Gallery at Elmira College.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "55 Mercer" is filed here.

did.unittitle: Frances Lehman Loeb Art Center-Vassar College

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: In the "Monumental Sculpture" booklet on page 9 is the sculpture "Song of the Vowels" by Jacques Lipchitz, 1931-1932, which is now in front of Cornell's Uris Library.

did.unittitle: Hamilton College-Edward W. Root Art Center

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains a pamphlet for an exhibition of art by Cornell University faculty.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains a catalogue of historic city plan facsimiles available for purchase.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains exhibition booklet signed by Bertha Urdang.

did.unittitle: IBM Gallery of Science and Art
Institute for Architecture and Urban Studies
International Centre for the Study of the Preservation and Restoration of Cultural Property
Isabella Stewart Gardner Museum Fenway Court
Los Angeles County Museum of Art
Louisville, University of-Allen R. Hite Art Institute
Miscellaneous ads, letters, and printed material

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This folder contains an assortment of promotional ads and pamphlets, along with material concerning the improvement and maintenance of towns, from artchitecural renderings to landmark conservation information. Receipts, letters, and the libretto Capriccio by Richard Strauss are also enclosed.

did.unittitle: National Collection of Fine Arts, Smithsonian

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Information about the inventory of American Sculpture and research resources in the Library of the National Museum of American Art is enclosed.

did.unittitle: New York State Council on the Arts

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two of three items are without a date.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes profiles of the 32 Masters of Fine Arts Graduates of the Otis Art Institute class of 1970.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains an annotated 1953 exhibition and price catalogue.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains an "Eigth Annual Philadephia Water Color Exhibition Catlogue" from 1910.

did.unittitle: Series VIII. New York Urban Planning

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains historical information and urban planning information about cities and towns in New York state. The contents include town museum pamphlets and booklets, pamphlets with city walking tours often focusing on architecture, information about historical museums, and newspaper clippings about construction and restoration projects.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Though most of these articles concern restoration and demolition of public buildings, this file also contains Cornell-related articles that do not focus on Ithaca buildings.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: These newspaper clippings chronicle the potential demolition, movement for restoration, and subsequent restoration of the Clinton House.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series concerns subject-specific architecture and design. It contains promotional materials, articles, letters, guidebooks, and urban planning information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains pamphlets advertising geodesic domes, as first introduced by R. Buckminster Fuller.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains a lawsuit transcript and various anti-fast food letters and articles.
